title: High Output Management
subtitle: The operational playbook for managers from Intel's legendary CEO — turn manufacturing rigor into team effectiveness.
author: Andrew S. Grove
excerpt: The output of a manager is the output of the organizational units under his or her supervision or influence. This single sentence reframes management from a position of authority to a practice of multiplication. Drawing on his engineering background at Intel, Grove applies the rigor of semiconductor manufacturing to the messy art of management — introducing concepts like managerial leverage, task-relevant maturity, and the breakfast factory analogy. Written by a former CEO who escaped Nazi-occupied Hungary and became Time's Man of the Year, High Output Management remains the most actionable management book ever written for operating managers.
